chunks.title: Windy Ridge philosophy
chunks.text: Windy Ridge Chiropractic helps people move better, feel better, and get back to life unrestricted. Wendy should sound practical, warm, locally grounded, and focused on active Montana life in Bozeman and Big Sky. Care is conservative and evaluation-based, with no diagnosis or promised outcomes in chat.

chunks.title: Safety and red-flag guidance
chunks.text: Urgent or severe symptoms should be directed to medical care right away. Red flags include severe or rapidly worsening pain, major trauma, chest pain, trouble breathing, sudden weakness, numbness in the groin or saddle area, loss of bowel or bladder control, fainting, stroke-like symptoms, fever with severe symptoms, or concerning animal symptoms. Wendy should not add booking or sales CTAs for urgent red-flag situations.

chunks.title: Mr. James Beaudry
chunks.text: Mr. James Beaudry Credentials/suffix: LMT Disciplines: Massage Therapy Bio summary: James Beaudry became a massage therapist after experiencing the powerful benefits of treatment firsthand while recovering from injuries sustained through manual labor and powerlifting. His personal journey sparked a passion for helping others regain mobility and live pain-free lives. Over the past six years, James has worked closely with Physical Therapists and Chiropractors, gaining valuable mentorship and refining his evidence-based approach to care. He integrates a range of techniques, including Swedish Massage, Deep Tissue, Myofascial Release (MFR), Neuromuscular Therapy, Cupping, Kinesio Taping, Graston, and Muscle Energy Technique (MET), to reduce pain and support long-term healing and

chunks.title: Dr. David Dalgardno
chunks.text: Dr. David Dalgardno Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Dr. David Dalgardno’s passion for helping injured individuals return to their active lives began in his working-class hometown of Longview, Washington, where he witnessed injuries among his friends and experienced his own. This led him to pursue chiropractic care, culminating in his graduation from the University of Western States in Portland, Oregon, in 2011. Dr. David practiced in Longview for six years, aiding countless patients in their recovery and return to activities they loved. In early 2017, he relocated to Bozeman, Montana, drawn by his love for outdoor activities like snow sports, mountain biking, fishing, and hunting. His mission is to help patients re-engage with activities that

chunks.title: Dr. Kyle Delamielleure
chunks.text: Dr. Kyle Delamielleure Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Dr. Kyle DeLamielleure is a third-generation Doctor of Chiropractic. Originally from Grand Rapids, Michigan, he spent much of his time in the North Woods of Cheboygan and Marquette before moving west. Drawn to Montana by its mountains, wide-open spaces, and strong sense of community, he is proud to build his life and practice here. Dr. Kyle is passionate about helping a wide range of patients, from athletes and weekend warriors to individuals simply looking to feel and function at their best. His mission is to help people regain control of their health, restore function, and return to the activities they love. With special interests in the neurology of chiropractic, Dr. Kyle combines precise

chunks.title: Dr. Joshua Prange
chunks.text: Dr. Joshua Prange Disciplines: Chiropractic, Veterinary Chiropractic Adjustment Bio summary: Dr. Josh was born and raised in St. Louis, MO. He attended Westminster College, where he earned his bachelor’s degree in Biology. Following his undergraduate studies, he pursued his chiropractic education at Logan College of Chiropractic, obtaining his Doctorate of Chiropractic and a Master of Science in Exercise Prescription and Sports Rehab. Upon graduation, Dr. Josh moved to Fairbanks, AK, where he practiced until 2013. In that year, he and his wife relocated to the Bozeman/Belgrade area to raise their growing family. He has been serving the Gallatin Valley for the past decade, driven by his love for the outdoors and animals. Dr. Josh’s passion for continuous learning led him to graduate

chunks.title: Dr. Claire Schauf
chunks.text: Dr. Claire Schauf Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Dr. Claire’s love for chiropractic began after her undergraduate studies at the University of St. Thomas in St. Paul, Minnesota, where working in a chiropractic office sparked her passion for supporting healthy beginnings from day one. Inspired by the body’s natural ability to heal, she went on to earn her degree from Northwestern Health Sciences University and later moved to Bozeman, Montana, to complete her training and begin her career. She now specializes in pregnancy, postpartum, and pediatric care, using a variety of techniques to help each patient feel supported and cared for while focusing on their whole health and the meaningful changes chiropractic care can create.

chunks.title: Dr. Michelle Snider
chunks.text: Dr. Michelle Snider Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Originally from Keystone, Colorado, Dr. Michelle grew up ski racing, where frequent injuries sparked her journey in becoming a Chiropractor. She earned her Doctor of Chiropractic degree from the University of Western States. After a decade of moving around the country with her Navy husband, she’s excited to call Montana home and join the Windy Ridge Chiropractic team. Dr. Michelle provides compassionate, family-focused care for patients of all ages — from newborns and expectant mothers to athletes and active families. She personally tailors each visit to her patient’s needs using soft tissue techniques, cranial work, and a wide range of adjusting styles, including low-force methods. She is a ---

chunks.title: 30 Min Pre-Chiropractic Warm Up Massage
chunks.text: 30 Min Pre-Chiropractic Warm Up Massage Discipline: Massage Therapy Duration: 30 minutes Scheduled time: 30 minutes Current listed price: Call to book; pricing may need confirmation Providers: Nichole Wambsgans Booking type: Call to book Description: 30-Minute Pre-Chiro Focused Massage with Nichole: This targeted 30-minute session is designed to complement your chiropractic care by preparing specific areas of the body for optimal adjustment. Nichole uses focused techniques to release tension, improve tissue pliability, and promote circulation in the regions most in need—typically the neck, back, hips, or shoulders. Exclusively offered as a pre-adjustment add-on, this massage is ideal for patients seeking a more relaxed, responsive body going into their chiropractic session. Note: This is not a full-body massage. It is a concentrated, therapeutic session tailored to enhance your chiropractic visit.

chunks.title: 60 Min Therapeutic Massage- Big Sky
chunks.text: 60 Min Therapeutic Massage- Big Sky Discipline: Massage Therapy Duration: 60 minutes Scheduled time: 65 minutes Current listed price: $155.00 Providers: Nichole Wambsgans Booking type: Online bookable Description: 60-Minute Massage – Full Body Therapeutic Myofascial Release This full-body session blends therapeutic massage with targeted myofascial release techniques to reduce pain, restore movement, and support long-term recovery. Using slow, intentional pressure and sustained holds, your therapist works through the fascial system—releasing restrictions, easing tension, and improving tissue health. Ideal for chronic tightness, postural strain, or athletic recovery, this treatment is deeply restorative and tailored to your body’s needs.

chunks.title: Dr. David Dalgardno
chunks.text: Dr. David Dalgardno Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Dr. David Dalgardno’s passion for helping injured individuals return to their active lives began in his working-class hometown of Longview, Washington, where he witnessed injuries among his friends and experienced his own. This led him to pursue chiropractic care, culminating in his graduation from the University of Western States in Portland, Oregon, in 2011. Dr. David practiced in Longview for six years, aiding countless patients in their recovery and return to activities they loved. In early 2017, he relocated to Bozeman, Montana, drawn by his love for outdoor activities like snow sports, mountain biking, fishing, and hunting. His mission is to help patients re-engage with activities that

chunks.title: Dr. Kyle Delamielleure
chunks.text: Dr. Kyle Delamielleure Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Dr. Kyle DeLamielleure is a third-generation Doctor of Chiropractic. Originally from Grand Rapids, Michigan, he spent much of his time in the North Woods of Cheboygan and Marquette before moving west. Drawn to Montana by its mountains, wide-open spaces, and strong sense of community, he is proud to build his life and practice here. Dr. Kyle is passionate about helping a wide range of patients, from athletes and weekend warriors to individuals simply looking to feel and function at their best. His mission is to help people regain control of their health, restore function, and return to the activities they love. With special interests in the neurology of chiropractic, Dr. Kyle combines precise

chunks.title: Dr. Michelle Snider
chunks.text: Dr. Michelle Snider Disciplines: Chiropractic Bio summary: Originally from Keystone, Colorado, Dr. Michelle grew up ski racing, where frequent injuries sparked her journey in becoming a Chiropractor. She earned her Doctor of Chiropractic degree from the University of Western States. After a decade of moving around the country with her Navy husband, she’s excited to call Montana home and join the Windy Ridge Chiropractic team. Dr. Michelle provides compassionate, family-focused care for patients of all ages — from newborns and expectant mothers to athletes and active families. chunks.title: Nichole Wambsgans
chunks.text: Nichole Wambsgans Disciplines: Massage Therapy Bio summary: With over 15 years of hands-on experience, Nichole specializes in deep tissue, myofascial release, and trigger point therapy. Trained at Alaska Career College, her approach blends clinical skill with compassionate care. Originally from Nebraska and raised in a military family, Nichole spent much of her career in the Southeast focusing on medical massage. Inspired by a life of service, she brings deep empathy and a strong work ethic—especially when working with veterans and active individuals. Now rooted in Montana with her family and daughter attending MSU, Nichole is proud to offer focused, therapeutic care in a place that feels like home. chunks.title: Pediatric Chiropractic Care
chunks.text: Pediatric Chiropractic Care URL: Priority score: 4 Pediatric Chiropractic Care Pediatric chiropractor Bozeman families trust should offer care that is gentle, specific, and designed around how children grow and develop. At Windy Ridge Chiropractic, pediatric care focuses on helping kids move well, regulate well, and adapt well as their bodies and nervous systems change. Children are constantly growing, learning, and responding to the world around them. From birth and early development to school, sports, and outdoor activity, their bodies take in a tremendous amount of physical and sensory input. Sometimes that stress shows up as tension, restricted movement, trouble settling, sleep challenges, or patterns that simply feel “off” to parents. Pediatric chiropractic care is not just about pain. It is about supporting healthy function, movement, and nervous system regulation so children can grow from a stronger foundation. Why Parents Seek Pediatric Chiropractic Care Children are constantly growing, learning, and adapting to the world around them. From early development through school, sports, and outdoor activity, their bodies are exposed to a wide range of physical and sensory stress. Over time, that stress can show up as tension, restricted movement, sleep challenges, or patterns that simply feel “off” to parents. It is about supporting healthy movement, function, and nervous system regulation so children can grow from a stronger foundation. Postural strain from school, sports, or screen time Tension or stiffness affecting movement Difficulty relaxing or settling Sleep challenges General irritability or restlessness Support during rapid growth and development Children may not always communicate what they are feeling clearly, but parents often notice subtle changes. chunks.title: Prenatal Chiropractic Care
chunks.text: Prenatal Chiropractic Care URL: Priority score: 4 Prenatal Chiropractic Care Many women searching for a pregnancy chiropractor in Bozeman are looking for a safe, natural way to stay comfortable as their body changes. As pregnancy progresses, the spine and pelvis adapt to support a growing baby. These changes can create tension, altered movement, and added stress on the body. Gentle prenatal chiropractic care focuses on restoring motion, reducing strain, and supporting both comfort and nervous system balance throughout pregnancy. Why Consider Chiropractic Care During Pregnancy Pregnancy places unique demands on the body. As the baby grows, posture shifts, weight distribution changes, and the pelvis must adapt to support both movement and development. These changes are normal, but they can also lead to tension, stiffness, and discomfort—especially in the low back, hips, and pelvis. Chiropractic care during pregnancy focuses on helping the body adapt more efficiently to these changes by improving movement, reducing mechanical stress, and supporting overall function. How Pregnancy Affects the Spine and Pelvis As pregnancy progresses, the center of gravity shifts forward. This often increases stress on the lumbar spine and changes how the pelvis moves and stabilizes. Increased ligament laxity allows the body to prepare for delivery, but it can also lead to instability and uneven movement patterns. When joints are not moving well or muscles become overly tight, this can contribute to discomfort and inefficient movement patterns throughout the body. The Webster Technique is a specific chiropractic approach used during pregnancy that focuses on the pelvis and surrounding structures. The goal is to improve balance within the pelvis, reduce tension, and support optimal positionin

chunks.title: BIG SKY, MT
chunks.text: BIG SKY, MT URL: Priority score: 2 BIG SKY, MT Windy Ridge Chiropractic in Big Sky, Montana provides hands-on care for residents, seasonal visitors, and mountain athletes looking to stay active and pain-free. As a trusted Big Sky chiropractor located in Town Center near the base of Lone Peak, we help patients recover from ski injuries, reduce back and neck pain, improve mobility, and maintain performance in the mountains. Our approach focuses on identifying the root cause of dysfunction, not just treating symptoms, so you can return to activity with confidence. Whether you live here year-round or are visiting for skiing, hiking, or mountain biking, our care is tailored to keep you moving and performing at a high level in the Big Sky environment. 9 average rating · 56 reviews Read all reviews on Google Dae M. 3 weeks ago ★★★★★ Great experience! Had a rather complex cervicogenic vertigo/dizziness and ear rocks issue combined with rotator cuff PRP recovery and Dr Dave’s (and Dr Snider’s) experience, bedside manner and skill really saved me. Owner response: Thanks for the kind words Dae! It was so great to see you the other day, looks like you’re thriving again. Michelle and Nicole (Massage Therapist)! I've been having horrible sciatica pain and all three of them have been helping me for the past several weeks. My pain was 12/10 when I started with them and today I can say it's 1/10 thanks to their excellent care! I highly recommend all three of them! 3 months ago ★★★★★ I can’t say enough good things about Dr. I came to her after a leg injury I sustained while training for a half marathon, and she helped me recover faster than I ever thought possible. She took the time to understand exactly what was going on. Owner response: Thank you so much for taking the time to share th

chunks.title: Our Chiropractic Team
chunks.text: Our Chiropractic Team URL: Priority score: 2 Our Chiropractic Team Meet our Doctors Dr. David Dalgardno's journey to becoming a chiropractor began in Longview, Washington, where early experiences with injury kindled his desire to heal. Admitted to the University of Western States after rigorous study, he graduated in 2011 and practiced in his hometown, helping patients regain their active lifestyles. In 2017, he moved to Bozeman, Montana, to embrace mountain life fully. An avid outdoorsman and musician, Dr. David leverages his diverse interests to enrich his therapeutic approach, promoting healing through both his clinical practice and personal passions, aiming to improve patients' quality of life by reconnecting them with their cherished activities. Josh Prange, DC Born and raised in St. Josh pursued his academic journey at Westminster College, achieving a bachelor's degree in Biology. He further enhanced his education at Logan College of Chiropractic, where he earned both a Doctorate of Chiropractic and a Master of Science in Exercise Prescription and Sports Rehab. Upon completing his studies in 2010, Dr. Josh practiced in Fairbanks, AK, until 2013, when he moved to the Bozeman/Belgrade area in Montana to raise his family and continue his practice. With a deep passion for the outdoors and animals, he has been a dedicated practitioner in the Gallatin Valley for over ten years. Michelle Snider, DC Originally from Keystone, Colorado, Dr. Michelle grew up ski racing, where frequent injuries sparked her journey in becoming a Chiropractor. She earned her Doctor of Chiropractic degree from the University of Western States. After a decade of moving around the country with her Navy husband, she’s excited to call Montana home. Michelle provides compassionate, family-focused car

chunks.text: Delanee Hicks URL: Priority score: 2 Delanee Hicks OFFICE COORDINATOR · WINDY RIDGE CHIROPRACTIC PATIENT EXPERIENCE · ORGANIZATION · SUPPORT Helping patients feel welcomed, informed, and supported from their very first interaction with the clinic. Delanee plays a key role in creating a smooth and comfortable experience, making sure patients know what to expect and feel taken care of every step of the way. She helps keep everything organized behind the scenes so patients can focus on their care and getting back to what they love. SCHEDULE AN APPOINTMENT ABOUT DELANEE Delanee is the Administrative Director at Windy Ridge Chiropractic and is often one of the first people patients connect with when they reach out to the clinic. From the moment someone calls, messages, or walks through the door, she helps set the tone for the entire experience by making sure patients feel welcomed, informed, and comfortable from the very beginning. Her role is centered around helping patients navigate the process of starting care with as little stress and confusion as possible. Whether someone is booking their first appointment, asking questions about what to expect, or trying to understand the next steps in their care, Delanee helps make sure they feel supported and taken care of along the way. She also plays an important role in helping Windy Ridge maintain the kind of clinic environment patients appreciate: friendly, organized, responsive, and focused on making high quality care feel approachable. You can also meet the rest of our team here . chunks.title: Dr. Claire Schauf, D.C.
chunks.text: Dr. Claire Schauf, D.C. URL: Priority score: 2 PREGNANCY & PEDIATRIC CHIROPRACTOR · WINDY RIDGE CHIROPRACTIC PEDIATRIC FOCUSED CARE Helping moms, babies, and growing families feel supported, comfortable, and confident through every stage of care. Claire specializes in pregnancy, postpartum, and pediatric chiropractic, using gentle, personalized techniques designed to support both the body and the experience of care. Known for creating a calm and welcoming environment, patients often leave feeling more relaxed, more confident, and better supported in caring for themselves and their family. Claire’s passion for chiropractic care is rooted in her commitment to supporting healthy beginnings for both moms and babies. She was drawn to this work through a deep appreciation for how early life experiences can shape long-term health, and the opportunity to positively influence that process through thoughtful, hands-on care. Her focus is centered around helping women feel more confident and supported throughout pregnancy, while also creating a foundation for infants and children to grow and develop with less restriction and stress on their bodies. She values the role chiropractic care can play not only in managing discomfort, but in helping families feel more connected to their health from the very beginning. Claire prioritizes creating an environment where patients feel comfortable, heard, and unrushed. chunks.title: Dr. David Dalgardno, D.C.
chunks.text: Dr. David Dalgardno, D.C. URL: Priority score: 2 FOUNDER · WINDY RIDGE CHIROPRACTIC LIFE. Helping active individuals move better, recover from injury, and stay out of the cycle of recurring pain. David works with people who want to stay active, keep up with their lifestyle, and stop being limited by pain or restriction. Known for clear explanations and results-driven care, patients leave understanding what’s going on and what needs to happen next. David built Windy Ridge Chiropractic with a clear goal in mind: create a clinic experience that feels different from what most people expect in healthcare. Less rushed, more thoughtful, and centered on helping people actually move forward instead of feeling like they are being pushed through a system. His work is rooted in helping active individuals stay active. That often means treating people who are balancing work, family, and the constant pull of daily life while still wanting to ski, hike, train, lift, or simply feel good enough to enjoy where they live. Many of the patients he works with are not trying to become someone new. They are trying to get back to being themselves again. That perspective has shaped Windy Ridge into a clinic focused on clarity, function, and real-world results. If you want to meet the rest of the team supporting that mission, you can view our Bozeman chiropractic team here . WHAT HE HELPS WITH Most people initially come in because something hurts. David focuses on is understanding why it keeps coming back and why previous care may not have created lasting change. His goal is to help patients break out of that cycle instead of constantly chasing symptoms from flare-up to flare-up. He commonly works with low back and SI joint pain, neck pain, headaches, upper back tension, and recurring movement-bas

chunks.title: Dr. Joshua Prange, D.C.
chunks.text: Dr. Joshua Prange, D.C. URL: Priority score: 2 CHIEF OPERATIONS OFFICER · WINDY RIDGE CHIROPRACTIC ANIMAL CHIROPRACTIC Helping athletes, active individuals, and animals recover from injury and get back to doing what they love. Josh focuses on sports injuries, disc-related conditions, and full-body musculoskeletal care with a practical, results-driven approach built around each patient’s goals. Known for his ability to clearly explain what is going on and actually fix the problem, patients often leave saying, “that feels so much better” or “I wish I would have known about this sooner. Josh built his academic foundation in the sciences before pursuing a career centered on movement, recovery, and helping others live more actively. He earned his bachelor’s degree in Biology from Westminster College and went on to continue his education at Logan College of Chiropractic. There, he completed both his Doctorate of Chiropractic and a Master of Science in Exercise Prescription and Sports Rehabilitation. That combination still shapes how he practices today. His background gives him a strong understanding not only of pain and injury, but of how the body performs, adapts, and gets stronger over time. After finishing school in 2010, Dr. Josh practiced in Fairbanks, Alaska before eventually making his way to the Bozeman and Belgrade area in 2013. Since then, he has spent more than a decade serving patients in the Gallatin Valley while building a life centered around family, the outdoors, and the Montana lifestyle. Josh helps a wide range of patients, from athletes and active adults to ranch and working animals. You can also meet the rest of our team here . Josh brings a mix of experience, personality, and clear communication into every visit. He is the kind of doctor who takes the tim

chunks.title: Dr. Kyle DeLamielleure, D.C.
chunks.text: Dr. Kyle DeLamielleure, D.C. URL: Priority score: 2 BOZEMAN CHIROPRACTOR FOR ACTIVE LIFESTYLES PERFORMANCE · FUNCTION · RECOVERY Helping athletes and active individuals recover, move better, and keep doing what they love at a higher level. Kyle combines chiropractic adjustments, dry needling, and soft tissue work with a performance-focused approach built around how you move and what matters most to you. Patients often describe him as a strong combination of the other providers at Windy Ridge, with care that is practical, adaptable, and simply gets it done. Kyle enjoys working with athletes and with anyone who wants more out of their body. His care is especially geared toward people who do not want to feel limited by pain, stiffness, recurring injuries, or the sense that their body is holding them back from the life they want to live. That often includes people who care deeply about staying active, performing well, recovering efficiently, and continuing to do the things they love for the long haul. In Bozeman, that may mean staying strong enough for the mountains, resilient enough for training, or simply capable enough to keep up with work, family, and the outdoors without constantly feeling restricted. While he works with a wide range of conditions, his favorite cases are often performance-focused. These are the patients who are not just trying to get out of pain, but who want to move better, recover smarter, and perform at a higher level with more confidence in their body. If you want to meet the rest of the team at Windy Ridge, you can view our team here . WHAT A VISIT LOOKS LIKE A typical visit starts with a full exam and a real conversation about you, your goals, and what matters most to you physically. chunks.title: Dr. Michelle Snider, D.C.
chunks.text: Dr. Michelle Snider, D.C. URL: Priority score: 2 PREGNANCY & 038; PEDIATRIC CHIROPRACTOR · WINDY RIDGE CHIROPRACTIC WEBSTER CERTIFIED · FAMILY-FOCUSED CARE Helping moms, babies, and families feel supported, understood, and cared for through every stage of life. Michelle provides compassionate, individualized care for pregnancy, postpartum, pediatric, and active family lifestyles using a combination of gentle techniques, soft tissue work, and cranial approaches. Known for taking time with each patient, she creates an environment where people feel unrushed, fully heard, and confident in their care. Michelle’s path into chiropractic care began through her own experience as a competitive ski racer. After dealing with multiple injuries throughout her athletic career, she was introduced to a form of care that went beyond simple symptom relief. Her treatment included not only adjustments, but also soft tissue work, recovery strategies, and nutritional support. That experience shaped how she views health today: as something that involves the entire body, not just isolated symptoms. During her undergraduate studies, she worked in a sleep research lab studying sleep deprivation and circadian rhythms. While she enjoyed the scientific side of research, she realized she wanted to work directly with people and have a more immediate impact on their health and daily life. Michelle combines her clinical training with a whole-body, patient-centered approach. She works with individuals and families to better understand what is happening in their body and create care plans that support long-term health and function. You can also meet the rest of our team here . Michelle approaches care with the understanding that no two patients are the same. Every visit begins with a detailed history and

chunks.title: Chiropractic Services
chunks.text: Chiropractic Services URL: Priority score: 2 Chiropractic Services We offer comprehensive chiropractic services designed to help individuals and families move better, feel better, and stay active. Care at Windy Ridge Chiropractic begins with a focused evaluation to identify the root cause of your symptoms and guide a treatment plan tailored to your goals. Treatment may include chiropractic adjustments, the Webster Technique for pregnancy , dry needling therapy, instrument-assisted soft tissue mobilization (IASTM), functional movement taping, and personalized care plans that support recovery and long-term health. Our clinic focuses on whole-family care , providing chiropractic services for adults, athletes, pregnancy, and pediatric patients so every stage of life can benefit from healthy movement and effective care. Make an Appointment Diversified Adjusting Diversified chiropractic manipulation helps to improve the alignment and motion of the spine and extremities. Soft tissue therapies including Dry needling, Graston, and trigger point work help to support chiropractic adjusting by improving range of motion, and increasing blood flow to injured tissues. We use chiropractic adjusting, soft tissue therapies, and lifestyle modification to alleviate pain, increase function, and improve overall quality of life. Read more Instrument Assisted Soft Tissue Manipulation This is a soft tissue therapy using specially shaped instruments to smooth scarred or knotted muscle and fascial tissue. This therapy is great for post surgical scars, chronic injuries, and even chronic pain. Read more Dry Needling Therapy This is a therapy involving inserting fine, solid needles into tight, inflamed, or knotted tissues inorder to relax and improve blood flow. This can be the game changer for long

chunks.title: Chiropractic Massage
chunks.text: Chiropractic Massage URL: Priority score: 2 Chiropractic Massage Chiropractic massage plays an important role in relieving tension, improving circulation, and supporting overall wellness. When combined with chiropractic care, massage can help your body heal more efficiently and move more comfortably. At Windy Ridge Chiropractic, we offer therapeutic bodywork provided by licensed massage therapists. Our focus is purposeful, individualized care designed to support your body’s healing and recovery. Therapeutic massage is well suited for athletes, weekend warriors, individuals recovering from injury, and anyone dealing with muscle tension or restricted movement. Whether your goal is pain reduction, improved mobility, or long-term soft tissue health, our massage therapists can help. Techniques and tools are selected based on your specific needs, ensuring each treatment is tailored to what your body requires that day. Working alongside our chiropractors , therapeutic massage is integrated into your overall care when appropriate. Our team collaborates to create a cohesive plan that supports your goals, helps you feel better, and allows you to live Life. By continuing to browse this site, you agree to our privacy-policy . chunks.title: Cupping Therapy
chunks.text: Cupping Therapy URL: Priority score: 2 Cupping Therapy Cupping therapy is a soft tissue therapy involving placing specially designed cups on the skin, and evacuating some of the air in order to draw the soft tissue up into the cup. Doing this creates lift, which pulls fluids through the tissue, increasing blood flow to the local area, and decompressing adhesions within the fascia between the skin and underlying tissues. From professional athletes after a session with an athletic trainer, to stay at home moms coming out of a well-deserved massage, the circular red or purple bruises seem to be everywhere. Cupping therapy is a soft tissue therapy involving placing specially designed cups on the skin, and evacuating some of the air in order to draw the soft tissue up into the cup. Fascial tissues are connective tissues that provide support and structure to the body. When these tissues are damaged, they can cause stiffness, pain, and other problems. Cupping therapy is performed with a number of different methods. The original method applied by acupuncturists and practitioners of Chinese medicine involved the use of a thick glass bulb. Fire is applied to the inside of the bulb to heat the air, and the cup is then placed quickly onto the skin, which creates the vacuum when the air inside the cup cools. Modern cupping sets are made of plastic or glass, with a vacuum valve at one end, and a pumping device, designed to pull a specific amount of air from the cup. Other sets are made of heavy silicone, which when depressed push air from the cup, and when released, pull tissue into the cup. We use cupping to loosen chronically tight areas prior to adjusting, or in conjunction with other soft tissue therapies such as dry needling. We will apply multiple soft tissue therapies to an ar

chunks.title: Diversified Adjusting
chunks.text: Diversified Adjusting URL: Priority score: 2 Diversified Adjusting Diversified chiropractic manipulation helps to improve the alignment and motion of the spine and extremities. Soft tissue therapies including Dry needling, IASTM, and trigger point work help to support chiropractic adjusting by improving range of motion, and increasing blood flow to injured tissues. We use chiropractic adjusting, soft tissue therapies, and lifestyle modification to alleviate pain, increase function, and improve overall quality of life. “What Style of Chiropractic do you practice? ” We get the question all the time. In order to answer the question fully, let’s dig into the background of chiropractic medicine itself: The practice of chiropractic was founded on the belief that the body has the ability to heal itself and that the nervous system is the key to maintaining health. In the millenia before chiropractic, practitioners were known as "bone-setters. " They used manual adjustments to align the spine and other areas, and alleviate pain. The term "chiropractor" was not coined until relatively recently. The field of chiropractic has a rich history in America, dating back to the late 19th century. The first American chiropractor, DD Palmer, opened the first chiropractic school in 1895 in Davenport, Iowa. In the early 20th century, chiropractors faced significant opposition from the medical community, but over time, and with improvements in both research and education, the practice has grown in popularity and acceptance by the wider medical community. Today, chiropractic is considered a mainstream form of healthcare and is widely accepted. At Windy Ridge Chiropractic in Bozeman, MT, we use a technique called "Diversified Technique" which allows us to customize treatment to the full spectrum

chunks.title: Dry Needling Therapy
chunks.text: Dry Needling Therapy URL: Priority score: 2 Dry Needling Therapy This is a therapy involving inserting fine, solid needles into tight, inflamed, or knotted tissues in order to relax and improve blood flow. This can be the game changer for long-term soft tissueconditions that have failed to respond to exercise, stretching, or adjusting alone. Injuries to soft tissue can be frustrating. Seemingly out of nowhere, constant nagging pain can occur. This can be especially concerning when stretching, rest, massage and other self-care strategies seem to be ineffective. Patients with these types of concerns are frequently seen here at the clinic. An investigation into the history of the pain often reveals auto accidents, postural strain, and sports injuries as the cause for long-term soft tissue pain. A custom-fit approach to addressing the needs of these patients is the best way to help them get back to enjoying life without pain. Dry needling is just one such tool that we add to our toolbox to accomplish this. It is important to note that dry needling is not the same as acupuncture, which is a form of Chinese medicine that involves the insertion of needles into specific points on the body for the purpose of balancing the flow of energy through the body. Dry needling is a form of soft tissue therapy treatment that involves the insertion of thin needles into specific points on the body, without the use of medication or injection. Dry needling is commonly used to treat musculoskeletal conditions such as back pain, neck pain, and muscle spasms. It may also be used to treat headaches, tendonitis, and other conditions. During a treatment, needles are typically left in place for a few minutes before being removed. Sometimes the area is moved passively or actively, or electronic muscle

chunks.title: Functional Movement Taping
chunks.text: Functional Movement Taping URL: Priority score: 2 Functional Movement Taping Functional movement taping is a great way to support your treatment. It alleviates pain, improves blood flow, and helps to reinforce your body’s natural stabilizing mechanisms to support what we are working on such as shoulders, spinal regions, hips, elbows knees, and other areas. Functional movement taping (FMT) is a technique used to support and stabilize the body during physical activity and rehabilitation. Kenzo Kase in the 1970s, this method uses special tape applied to the skin in specific patterns to mimic the body's natural tension lines. This can help to provide support and stability to the affected area, alleviate pain, improve blood flow, and reinforce the body's natural stabilizing mechanisms. FMT can be used for a variety of conditions, such as injuries to the shoulder, spine, hips, elbows, knees, and other areas. It is often used to help improve range of motion, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. FMT can be used in conjunction with other therapeutic modalities, such as exercise and manual therapy, to help optimize the body's function and improve overall physical performance. One of the key benefits of FMT is its ability to improve the body's natural movement patterns. The tape is applied to the skin in a specific pattern that mimics the natural tension lines of the body. This can help to provide support and stability to the affected area, allowing the body to move more efficiently and effectively. This is particularly useful for individuals who have suffered an injury or are recovering from surgery, as it can help to reduce pain and inflammation while promoting healing. FMT is also used to improve blood flow to the affected area. The tape is applied with an amount of tensio

chunks.title: IASTM Technique
chunks.text: IASTM Technique URL: Priority score: 2 IASTM Technique IASTM technique is a soft tissue therapy using specially shaped steel instruments to smooth scarred or knotted muscle and fascial tissue. This therapy is great for post surgical scars, chronic injuries, or even chronic pain. IASTM Technique is a form of instrument-assisted soft tissue mobilization that is becoming increasingly popular in the field of chiropractic care, and for good reason. This innovative technique is highly effective at breaking down scar tissue and other types of soft tissue adhesions, which can help to improve the overall function and range of motion of the affected joints and muscles. One of the reasons why IASTM Technique is so effective is that it is able to target the specific areas of the body that are affected by scar tissue and adhesions. The stainless steel instruments that are used in the technique are designed with specific curvatures that match the contours of the body, which allows practitioners to apply pressure to the affected areas with precision. This precision targeting helps to increase blood flow to the area, which can promote healing and reduce inflammation. In addition to its effectiveness, the IASTM Technique is also a non-invasive and drug-free form of treatment. Unlike surgery, the IASTM Technique does not require incisions or anesthesia, which means that patients experience minimal pain and discomfort during treatment. This makes it an ideal option for patients who are looking for an alternative to surgery or who are unable to undergo surgery due to other health conditions. One personal story that can be an example of the effectiveness of IASTM Technique is an experience of electricians and carpal tunnel. That’s why we’re committed to helping you get back to living life, unrestricted. Learn more Book an Appointment Unsure? Click Here to Take Our Quiz Why Choose Windy Ridge Chiropractic? At Windy Ridge Chiropractic, we focus on more than just temporary relief. Our goal is to help you move better, feel better, and stay active long-term. We work with athletes, active individuals, and families throughout Bozeman and Southwest Montana who want care that actually makes a difference. That means taking the time to understand what is driving your symptoms, using hands-on care to restore movement, and giving you clear guidance so you can keep doing what you love without constantly dealing with the same issues. DRY NEEDLING THERAPY This is a therapy involving inserting fine, solid needles into tight, inflamed, or knotted tissues in order to relax and improve blood flow. chunks.title: FAQ's
chunks.text: FAQ's URL: Priority score: 1 FAQ's Frequently Asked Questions At Windy Ridge Chiropractic, we understand that you may have questions about chiropractic care, our services, and how we can help you live a pain-free, active lifestyle. Below are some of the most common questions we receive, along with detailed answers to help you feel more informed and confident about your care. Chiropractic care is a non-invasive, hands-on health care discipline that focuses on the spine, muscles, joints, and nervous system. It uses manual adjustments and other techniques to help improve function, relieve pain, and promote overall health and wellness. Our goal is to help your body heal naturally, without medication or surgery. At Windy Ridge Chiropractic, we treat a variety of conditions, including but not limited to: Back and neck pain Headaches and migraines Shoulder, knee, and joint pain Sciatica Sports injuries Whiplash and auto accident injuries Postural issues General discomfort from daily activities If you’re dealing with pain or discomfort, we’re here to help you find relief and get back to enjoying life. What should I expect during my first visit? Your first visit to Windy Ridge Chiropractic will include a comprehensive assessment of your health and medical history. We’ll perform a thorough physical exam, discuss your symptoms, and determine the best course of treatment. If needed, we may refer to other outside providers, or order X-rays to better understand your condition. You’ll also receive your first adjustment at your first visit, whenever possible, and a tailored treatment plan. Chiropractic adjustments are generally not painful. Most patients report feeling immediate relief or a sense of release after an adjustment. In some cases, you may feel mild soreness similar to the s
